title: Quantification of Entanglement in Three-Qubit Systems
abstract: This study presents an analytical investigation of entanglement quantification in three-qubit pure states through the Acín canonical representation, which serves as a generalization of the two-qubit Schmidt decomposition. Driven by the intricacies of multipartite entanglement and the shortcomings of current measures, we employ a global concurrence measure derived from the generalized concurrence of various bipartitions within the system. The characteristics of this measure are explored both analytically and numerically across a range of SLOCC entanglement classes, such as product, biseparable, GHZ-type, and W-type states. When compared to the three-tangle and tripartite negativity, our findings indicate that this measure yields complementary insights into multipartite correlations. The outcomes underscore the significance of this measure in elucidating the structure and distribution of entanglement within three-qubit quantum systems.
summary: This paper introduces a global concurrence measure to quantify entanglement in three-qubit pure states by summing bipartite entanglement contributions across all possible partitions. Using the Acin canonical form, the authors demonstrate that this measure provides a broader view of total quantum correlations compared to measures sensitive only to genuine tripartite entanglement. It is a theoretical advancement for characterizing multipartite quantum states.
main_problem: The study addresses the complex quantification of multipartite entanglement in three-qubit pure states, aiming to overcome the limitations of existing entanglement measures.
main_result: The proposed global concurrence measure provides complementary insights into multipartite correlations, successfully distinguishing between different entanglement classes (e.g., GHZ vs. W states) where established measures might fail or only detect genuine entanglement.
method: The authors employ an analytical approach using the Acin canonical representation and define a global concurrence measure as the sum of generalized concurrences derived from the determinants of reduced density matrices of all three possible bipartitions.
model_or_system: The system under investigation is a three-qubit quantum system, whose pure states are parameterized using the Acin canonical representation, which generalizes the Schmidt decomposition.
key_observables: Global concurrence ($E$), three-tangle ($	au$), and tripartite negativity ($\mathcal{N}_3$).
important_parameters: The state is parameterized by five non-negative amplitudes ($\lambda_i$) and one phase ($\phi$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the global concurrence, constructed from bipartite correlations, is a suitable and comprehensive measure for total entanglement in three-qubit systems.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the variation of the global concurrence ($E$) across different entanglement classes (Biseparable, GHZ, W) and compare $E$ against $	au$ and $\mathcal{N}_3$ for both GHZ and W states.
paper_structure: The paper proceeds by first establishing the mathematical framework using the Acin canonical form, deriving the global concurrence measure $E$ from reduced density matrix determinants, and finally comparing $E$'s behavior against established measures like the three-tangle and tripartite negativity across various entanglement classes.
why_interesting: This work provides a robust, comprehensive tool ($E$) for quantifying total quantum correlations in small quantum systems, which is crucial for understanding resource theories in quantum information processing.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
